prompt:
Create a RoadSafe Inspection Operations platform that lets a regional vehicle inspection provider run its catalog, scheduling, and reporting fully offline on an internal network. The Vue.js web interface serves five primary roles: Administrators configure locations, policies, and user access; Data Engineers manage ingestion connectors; Coordinators schedule appointments and allocate resources; Inspectors conduct tests and publish results; Customers (walk-in or pre-registered) search eligible vehicle/test options and view their own reports. Users sign in with username and password and land on a dashboard showing today’s appointments, resource utilization, and ingestion job health. Coordinators manage a Test Center ledger with editable seat maps for waiting rooms and capacity for inspection bays and equipment; the system blocks overbooking by validating that each bay has one required equipment set and one assigned Inspector per 30-minute slot, and it can auto-allocate to the earliest available bay using rules such as “heavy-duty vehicles require Bay 3–6” and “emissions analyzer recalibration reserves 15 minutes after every 8 tests.” Customers and staff use advanced search to find vehicles and prior test records using keyword search plus combined filters such as brand, model year, price band (when applicable to inventory), energy type, and transmission; results paginate at 25 per page, support sorting by date or status, and provide autocomplete and trending keywords calculated from the last 7 days of local query logs. An in-app Messaging Center confirms appointments, records changes, announces report availability, and schedules follow-up reminders; optional SMS/email channel plugins may be installed but must operate in offline mode by writing messages to a local outbox for later manual delivery, while the in-app channel remains fully functional.
The backend uses Koa to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled Vue.js frontend, with MySQL as the system of record for users, roles, facilities/resources, appointments, inspection results, files, ingestion jobs, and audit events. Data ingestion runs through a plugin-based connector framework that supports CSV drops, local network shares, and device exports, orchestrated by a job queue with priorities, dependencies, incremental checkpoints, and backfill windows; incremental jobs run hourly by default, backfills are capped at 30 days per request, retries occur up to 5 times with exponential backoff, and a failed job can resume from the last committed checkpoint. ETL pipelines apply configurable parsing rules, field mapping, unit normalization (miles vs. kilometers), currency normalization to USD when ingesting pricing, entity alignment across sources, and deduplication using deterministic keys plus similarity thresholds; each run writes a versioned dataset with lineage so staff can trace a report back to source files and transforms, and quality metrics (missing fields, out-of-range values, duplicate rate) trigger in-app anomaly alerts when thresholds exceed 2% variance from the previous 14-run baseline. Security and compliance are enforced end-to-end: HTTPS with locally issued certificates encrypts data in transit, AES-256 encrypts sensitive columns at rest, privacy fields are masked on screen for non-privileged roles, and logs redact identifiers by default. Inputs are validated to prevent XSS/CSRF/SQL injection, passwords require at least 12 characters with complexity and are stored with salted hashing, API rate limits default to 60 requests/minute per user and 300/minute per IP, and privilege-escalation attempts are detected and written to immutable audit logs retained for 2 years with export to a local file. File uploads and downloads use authorization tokens and hotlink protection; “malware scanning” is implemented offline via strict allowlists, maximum file sizes (50 MB), MIME/extension validation, and hash-based blocking against an administrator-maintained denylist. Sensitive-content detection uses locally configured rules (regex/dictionary) to quarantine files containing patterns like SSNs, and data retention workflows retain inspection reports for 7 years then perform deletion with tombstoned references; account closure completes within 30 days with anonymization while preserving required compliance logs.
